elements.text: Midday heat bakes the Scarred Gorge, but the air remains freezing, choked by the oppressive, decaying aura of the ancient agent. Master Valerius’s lifeless body lies crumpled against a shattered spire, his blood still steaming on the dry earth.
We have to fall back, Kaelen! His sacrifice... it was meant to buy us a retreat, not a suicide. Stand up and draw your steel!

Kaelen does not move. He kneels by Valerius's body, his fingers tracing the strange, burning runes etched in the dirt. His eyes are wide, processing the raw, mathematical truth of the elder's final revelation.

He didn't die for a retreat, Lyra. He died to show me the frequency of the lock. The agent's power is not a solid wall; it is a system of pressure. To break it, I do not need more force. I need to balance the equation.
By doing what? Look at the sky—the corruption is crawling down the rock walls! We must purify it, or we die with our swords in our hands!
Purification is a slow, inefficient lie. To stop a flood, you do not build a dam of paper vows. You open the sluice gates.

Kaelen plunges his bare hands directly into the bubbling, black pool of corruption pooling around the Gorge's deepest fissure. The dark, oily liquid immediately crawls up his forearms, hissing as it burns his flesh.
No! Stop! It will eat your soul, Kaelen! That is the very filth we swore to destroy! There is no honor in becoming the monster!
Honor is a luxury reserved for those who survive to write the histories. I choose to write.

Kaelen pulls the corruption deep into his own meridians. His veins turn pitch black, visible through his skin like a web of cracked glass. He gasps, his jaw clenching as his body violently rejects, then forcibly assimilates, the ancient power.
You... you are disgusting. You have thrown away everything he died for.

A massive shockwave of dark, repurposed energy erupts from Kaelen, shattering the approaching tendrils of the ancient agent and forcing the invisible pressure to recede in a deafening howl. Kaelen stands, his eyes void of warmth, staring into the abyss of his own terrifying, new-found strength.
